data.title: Stock Market News Review: SPY Secures Record High on Unemployment Rate Drop and Rising Consumer Sentiment
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) finished the second week of 2026 on a high note, with the former clinching a new record-high close of $694.07.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This morning, the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) announced that the unemployment rate was 4.4% in December, below the consensus estimate of 4.5%. In addition, the BLS revised November's unemployment rate to 4.5% from 4.6%.

It wasn't all good news, as December's nonfarm payrolls clocked in at 50,000, which was below the estimate of 70,000. On top of that, October and November's payrolls were revised lower by a combined 76,000 jobs. For 2025, the U.S. economy added 584,000 jobs, registering the worst year for job gains outside of a recession since 2003.

"You don't want to become unemployed, because if you do, it's very difficult to get back in," said Moody's Analytics Chief Economist Mark Zandi. "The duration of unemployment is now becoming quite long." Rate cuts can stimulate the labor market, although the odds of a 25 bps rate cut at the January 28 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting more than halved to 5% from 11.1% a day ago on CME's FedWatch tool.

Elsewhere, the University of Michigan announced that January's preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment was 54, higher than the estimate of 53.5 and improving from 52.9 in December. At the same time, long-run inflation expectations rose by 0.2% to 3.4% and surpassed the range of 2.8% to 3.2% in 2025. Year-ahead inflation expectations remained unchanged at 4.2%, the lowest level since January 2025.

"Although consumers' worries about tariffs appear to be gradually receding, they remain guarded about the overall strength of business conditions and labor markets," said Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu.

Inflation is still a key risk for the economy, said Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta President Raphael Bostic in an interview with the WLRN radio station. Bostic noted that the Fed must remain "laser-focused" on lowering prices and previously stated that he would not support rate cuts in 2026 unless inflation eases.

Finally, investors expecting a Supreme Court ruling on the Trump administration's tariffs will have to wait a bit longer. SCOTUS held its opinion day at 10 a.m. Eastern Time, although the court didn't provide a decision on the case. SCOTUS has scheduled its next opinion day for Wednesday, January 14.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.65% gain, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) returned 1.02%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 3 High-Yield Pipeline Stocks to Watch as Oil Pressure Subsides: EPD, MPLX, and ET
data.description:
Brent crude is hovering around $61 a barrel today, with WTI near $56, following a gradual decline over recent years, while the EIA now expects most of 2026. We have inventories edging higher, supply growth still surprising to the upside, and on top of that, Washington has effectively taken control of Venezuela's export tap, authorizing sales of up to 50 million stranded barrels, adding another catalyst to keep prices depressed.

data.title: Data Center Spurt Suggests Strong Harvest for Seagate Technology (STX)
data.description:
Seagate Technology (STX), one of the world's leading data storage companies, has emerged as a major beneficiary of the AI boom, driven by accelerating enterprise demand for hard disk drives (HDDs). While the consumer storage market is now largely dominated by solid-state drives (SSDs), HDDs have reasserted their relevance in data centers due to their superior cost efficiency at scale.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Seagate represents a pure-play investment in the growth of HDD usage in enterprise and hyperscale environments, as the company remains almost entirely focused on HDDs. In contrast, its closest competitor, Western Digital Corporation (WDC), maintains a hybrid strategy across both HDDs and SSDs. I am bullish on Seagate's outlook and believe the company is attractively positioned at a forward P/E multiple of approximately 28x, particularly as its earnings growth is expected to accelerate meaningfully.
data.author: Dilantha De Silva

data.title: Mizuho Names Its Top 3 Chip Stocks for 2026
data.description:
Mizuho is positive on semiconductor stocks in 2026, driven by continued demand tied to artificial intelligence. In its latest outlook, the firm said AI-related chips and equipment should continue to support the sector, even as gains are expected to be smaller than last year. The firm named Nvidia (NVDA), Lumentum (LITE), and Broadcom (AVGO) as its "top stock picks for 2026," citing strength across AI accelerators, optical networking, and advanced chips.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Beyond its top picks, Mizuho also pointed to Microchip Technology (MCHP) and Lam Research (LRCX) as additional stocks to watch in 2026. The firm said demand for GPUs and custom AI chips remains strong, supporting Nvidia and Broadcom, while faster AI networking speeds such as 800G and 1.6T should benefit optical players like Lumentum.

Outside of AI chips, Mizuho sees wafer fab equipment and memory as key winners, helped by strong pricing in DRAM and NAND. The firm remains positive on Micron (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK), as demand tied to AI data centers continues to grow. That said, Mizuho struck a more cautious tone on autos, analog chips, PCs, and smartphones, citing softer demand and high inventory levels.

Overall, the firm believes semiconductor valuations remain attractive compared with the broader market, with AI-focused segments continuing to lead in 2026.

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Stellantis (STLA) to Stop Producing Jeep and Chrysler PHEVs in North America
data.description:
Automaker Stellantis (STLA) is planning to stop producing several of its plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEV) in North America. These include the Jeep Wrangler, Jeep Grand Cherokee, and Chrysler Pacifica, as demand for these models slows down. The company said that the decision is based on changing customer preferences and a need to focus on more competitive electrified options, such as traditional hybrids and range-extended vehicles. Therefore, Stellantis will begin phasing out its plug-in hybrid programs starting with the 2026 model year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This move is a major change from just a year ago, when Stellantis strongly promoted these vehicles as market leaders. In 2024, Jeep expected to sell between 160,000 and 170,000 plug-in hybrids, and the company said that those models made up 41% of all U.S. PHEV sales. In addition to sales, Stellantis used plug-in hybrids to help balance out emissions from its large gas-powered trucks and SUVs in order to meet federal fuel economy rules. However, that pressure has eased as the Trump administration reduced or removed parts of those regulations, thereby reducing the need for PHEVs to meet compliance targets.

At the same time, the Jeep plug-in models have faced problems, including a recent recall due to fire risk, although Stellantis said that the recall did not influence the decision to cancel the vehicles. Instead, Jeep CEO Bob Broderdorf told CNBC that the brand is reassessing its electrification plans after the end of up to $7,500 in federal incentives for EVs and PHEVs last September. While Stellantis will continue selling the plug-in hybrids already on dealer lots, Jeep will shift its focus toward fully electric SUVs like the Wagoneer S and Recon.

Is STLA Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on STLA stock based on eight Buys, eight Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average STLA price target of $11.51 per share implies that shares are trading near fair value.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Why is the Stock Market Up Today, 1/9/2026?
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are off to a strong start in 2026, with both indexes trading higher on Friday.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The market got a boost after the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reported that the unemployment rate in December was 4.4%, below the consensus estimate of 4.5%. In addition, the BLS revised November's unemployment rate to 4.5% from 4.6%.

Consumer Sentiment Tops Expectations

Furthermore, the University of Michigan's preliminary Index of Consumer Sentiment (ICS) for January was 54, higher than the consensus estimate of 53.5 and rising from 52.9 in December. At the same time, the reading is still down by 24.7% year-over-year from 71.7. A reading below 60 signals pessimism, while a reading above 70 signals optimism.

Still, the ICS has now risen for two consecutive months and is at its highest level since September 2025. Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu added that concerns about tariffs are beginning to recede, although consumers remain cautious about the state of the labor market.

data.title: ATZ Earnings: Canadian Retailer Aritzia Posts Record Revenue of $1.04 Billion
data.description:
Canadian clothing retailer Aritzia (ATZ), which is popular with teens and young adults, has reported financial results that surpassed the consensus estimate of analysts.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Vancouver-based company announced fiscal third-quarter earnings per share (EPS) of $1.10, which topped the $0.89 expected among analysts. The company's revenue in the quarter rose 43% to a record $1.04 billion, beating the $934.2 million forecast on Wall Street. It was the first time Aritzia's sales topped $1 billion.

Looking ahead, the company said it expects fiscal fourth-quarter revenue of $1.10 billion to $1.125 billion. Aritzia's gross margin is projected to be flat to up 50 basis points compared with the same quarter last year. Expenses as a percentage of revenue are expected to be flat to down 50 basis points from a year ago.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Johnson & Johnson Wins Tariff Relief from Trump in a Drug Pricing Deal
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) reached a significant agreement with the Trump administration to lower drug prices for Americans while securing exemptions from U.S. tariffs. The deal highlights the company's efforts to strike a balance between affordability for consumers and strategic business benefits.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This deal follows the Trump administration's similar drug-pricing agreements with other major pharmaceutical companies, including GSK (GSK), Merck (MRK), and Novartis (NVS).

More Details About the Deal

Johnson & Johnson said Thursday that its new agreement with the U.S. government meets the administration's requests and provides tariff exemptions for its pharmaceutical products. Specific details, including which drugs are affected or the exact price cuts, were not disclosed.

Under the deal, J&J will participate in TrumpRx.gov, letting Americans buy its medicines at significantly discounted rates and will offer Medicaid access at prices similar to those in other developed nations.

Notably, U.S. patients often pay nearly three times more for prescription drugs than people in other developed countries, and the Trump administration has been pushing drugmakers to lower prices. Since taking office last year, President Trump has repeatedly pushed for lower drug prices and threatened higher tariffs on imported pharmaceuticals to pressure drugmakers. Last year, he imposed a 100% tariff on all pharmaceutical imports but granted exemptions for companies investing in U.S. manufacturing facilities.

JNJ's Investment Plans

Johnson & Johnson also announced plans for two new U.S. manufacturing facilities as part of a $55 billion investment previously unveiled. Construction is underway on a $2 billion biologics plant in Wilson, N.C., which is expected to create around 5,000 skilled jobs. The company said it plans to announce further U.S. investments later this year.

Is JNJ a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, JNJ stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with 13 Buys and six Holds assigned in the last three months. JNJ's average stock price target is $219.05, suggesting a potential upside of 6.5% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: 'Still a Top Pick,' Says Analyst, Lifts Apple Stock Target to $330
data.description:
Wall Street is growing more confident in Apple's near-term outlook, supported by stronger-than-expected iPhone demand. Based on updated checks and industry data, top Evercore ISI analyst Amit Daryanani reiterated his Outperform rating on Apple (AAPL) and raised his price target to $330 from $325, implying about 25% upside from current levels.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It is worth noting that Daryanani ranks 184 out of more than 10,000 analysts tracked by TipRanks. He has a success rate of 62%, with an average return per rating of 19.7% over a one-year timeframe.

Why the Analyst Is Raising Estimates on Apple

The five-star analyst said iPhone demand is coming in stronger than expected, leading him to raise his estimates for the December quarter. He now expects revenue of $140.5 billion and earnings of $2.71 per share, both above Wall Street forecasts of $137.3 billion and $2.66, with iPhone growth running ahead of Apple's guidance for low double-digit growth.

The analyst also noted that Apple is mostly protected from higher memory costs in the near term, especially in the December and March quarters. As a result, stronger demand should flow through to profits rather than being offset by higher costs.

From a regional view, Daryanani sees solid iPhone demand in North America, China, and India, while Europe remains softer. He added that sales are shifting toward higher-end models, which supports better pricing and margins.

Looking ahead, the analyst said margins could face some pressure later in the year as contract negotiations continue, but he expects any impact to be manageable. Overall, he continues to see Apple as well positioned, supported by steady iPhone demand and favorable cost trends.

Is Apple a Buy or Sell Right Now? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on AAPL stock based on 19 Buys, 11 Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AAPL price target of $299.49 per share implies 15.62% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: FedEx Stock (FDX) Named a 'Top Pick' for 2026 at Bank of America
data.description:
The stock of Federal Express (FDX) has been upgraded and named a "Top Pick" for 2026 by Bank of America (BAC).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Shares of FedEx, as the company is commonly known, have been upgraded to Buy from Hold at Bank of America. The company is well-positioned to benefit from cost reductions, network consolidation, and a potential recovery from a four-year downturn in freight shipments, says the bank.

Analyst Ken Hoexter writes that the upgrade of FedEx is part of a broader shift in the bank's transportation strategy, with Bank of America bullish on "cyclical Transport stocks into 2026." Hoexter stresses that he expects a "freight recession" that began in 2022 to end this year.

The Improving Outlook for FedEx

The analyst also writes that demand for FedEx's shipping and logistics services should improve in 2026 as lower interest rates, infrastructure spending, and investment in data centres and power supply feed through the American economy.

Hoexter raised his price target on FDX stock to $365 from $315, and named FedEx one of Bank of America's "top picks for 2026." He also said that FedEx is gaining momentum from its ongoing cost-cutting strategy and yield discipline. Bank of America also points to the planned June 2026 spinoff of FedEx Freight as a key catalyst for the shipping giant.

Is FDX Stock a Buy?

FedEx stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 13 Buy, six Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average FDX price target of $315.05 implies 0.86% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole
